name: BernieSanders.com
subject: Their super PACs vs. our Super PACK
body:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ)

Sisters and Brothers -
Donald Trump has a super PAC. He also raised more than $40 million in the first three months of this year — more than double what we did. And we know that some of our Democratic opponents will have super PACs, too.
Well, we don’t want any part of that. Bernie’s not going to have a super PAC.
But what we do want to build is a Super PACK of supporters — the small percentage of people who give a monthly contribution to the campaign.
Monthly contributions are so critical to our campaign. The reason we’re asking you to give monthly is because it gives Bernie’s campaign a sustained, predictable source of revenue.
**Join Bernie’s Super PACK with a $5 monthly contribution to our campaign.**
[Donate: $5 monthly](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11512?refcode=em190505-2-15&amount=5&t=9&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ)
When you join Bernie’s Super PACK, we’ll send you this limited-time sticker for your phone, computer, car, or anywhere you want to put it to send a message that you’re part of the fight to take on the billionaire class of this country.
[](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11512?refcode=em190505-2-15-img&amount=5&t=10&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ)
Knowing that every month we'll be able to count on contributions from our Super PACK means that we can build the organization we need to win.
The billionaire class shouldn't be able to buy our democracy. But they are, and you can bet they’ll wade into this Democratic primary, as well.
But the good news is that together we are more powerful than they could possibly imagine.
**Can you join Bernie’s Super PACK with a contribution of $5 per month?**
[Donate: $5 monthly](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11512?refcode=em190505-2-15&amount=5&t=18&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ)
[_Or, make a monthly contribution of another amount._](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11512?refcode=em190505-2-15-other&t=19&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ)
Thank you for powering this campaign.
All my best,
Faiz Shakir
Campaign Manager

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=20&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=21&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=22&refcode2=1270_150750_EDhCpJ&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=23&akid=1270%2E150750%2EEDhCpJ).

body:
Statewide Digital Ad Launch

| | | | | | Team,
Today, our Campaign released a 30-second digital ad titled, "Outsider". The video highlights our goal to provide Mississippians with solutions to our state's problems of today and tomorrow.
The latest video is part of a series of statewide digital ads highlighting our political outsider, business and conservative background in Mississippi's Gubernatorial Primary this August, and we hope you will watch and share with your family and friends.
Our campaign is committed to reaching voters across Mississippi, whether in person or online, and we are utilizing every resource at our disposal to make sure Mississippians know that, as governor, I promise to move the needle on issues like education, infrastructure, and to make our state the best place to raise a family or hire an employee.

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I am writing to ask you to make a $3 contribution to our campaign. A group called the “Partnership for America’s Health Care Future” is running ads trying to stop us and our ideas. And I need your help to fight back.
body:
Here is the truth:
# [](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/8915?t=1&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ)

Sisters and Brothers -
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to make a donation to our campaign. I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why this request is so important — especially in light of some recent news.
The truth is, in this campaign we are not only taking on Donald Trump, his super PAC, and the huge fundraising head start he has amassed.
We are taking on very powerful corporate forces, like the insurance and pharmaceutical industries and their well-financed front-groups like the “Partnership for America’s Health Care Future.”
This group’s members aren’t patients or consumers or people impacted by our current health care system — they are insurance companies and the pharmaceutical industry’s lobbying group.
And they are already running very large advertising campaigns all over the country attacking us and the ideas that power our campaign – specifically, ideas like Medicare for All.
But let us be absolutely clear: These frantic attempts to derail our progress are a sign that we are winning. And that means we cannot rest, we cannot back down, and we cannot accept any substitute.
But I cannot do that alone. If we are going to overcome Trump’s big fundraising advantage and the insurance and drug industry fighting to stop us, I need your help.
So here is the part where I ask:
[**Can you make a $3 donation to our campaign today? I need your help fighting back against the insurance and drug companies spending big to defeat us, and to defeat our ideas.**](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190506-1-15&t=26&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ)
But if we are in this together, we are going to win.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.berniesanders.com/go/11505?refcode=em190506-1-15&t=27&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ)

Paid for by Bernie 2020
PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402
This email was sent to **dpwillis67@gmail.com**. If you need to, [**unsubscribe here**](https://bernie.actionkit.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=28&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ). You can also [sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=29&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ). We hope you'll stay, because emails are such an important part of how we organize and how we raise money. In fact, there is no single greater source for our campaign's fundraising than emails like this one. And that's important, because no one person, not even Bernie Sanders, can take on Trump and the billionaire class alone.

Before you go about the rest of your day, help power our fight against the billionaire class by [making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-email-template?refcode=em-footer&t=30&refcode2=1277_150750_DfxImQ&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ).
Submit this form to [update your contact information](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/update-info/?t=31&akid=1277%2E150750%2EDfxImQ).

But let us be absolutely clear: These frantic attempts to derail our progress are a sign that we are winning. And that means we cannot rest, we cannot back down, and we cannot accept any substitute. But I cannot do that alone. If we are going to overcome Trump’s big fundraising advantage and the insurance and drug industry fighting to stop us, I need your help. So here is the part where I ask: But if we are in this together, we are going to win. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders Paid for by Bernie 2020 PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

subject: This Economy Is Bad News for Socialists
body:
“The year 2019 was supposed to mark the end of the Trump economic surge. But the longer the year goes, the more it looks like there’s no end..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ddfd1acdd6&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **This is the Strongest Economy We’ve Seen in a Decade, America (That’s Bad News for Socialists)**

“The year 2019 was supposed to mark the end of the Trump economic surge. But the longer the year goes, the more it looks like there’s no end in sight. The economy is growing faster, job creation is resilient, and wage growth is surpassing anything we’ve seen in the last 10 years. It’s great news for the average American, and increasingly inconvenient for those telling us capitalism has failed,” Brian Brenberg writes in Fox News.

“Add it all up and it’s the strongest economic picture we’ve seen in more than a decade. And it’s left the gloom-and-doom prophets scratching their heads.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8f23d2eb22&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “It’s a good time not to be ‘privileged,’ to borrow the political vernacular of our progressive friends. Friday’s labor report showed employers added 263,000 jobs in April as the unemployment rate dropped to 3.6%, the lowest in five decades. The best news is that the biggest beneficiaries of this tight labor market are the folks who struggled during the slow-growth Obama years,” _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8311f52030&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “Many of the robust job gains came in businesses that hire those with less education. Construction added 33,000 jobs last month, for example, and has added 256,000 over the last year.”
---
|
---
| “If you relied on the Washington Post just a month before Election Day 2016, you would think the economy was on the brink of disaster,” Charles Gasparino writes in the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c809a430f2&e=3ee4985eea)_. “What’s even more egregious is that the same folks predicting the end of the world refuse to provide sane analysis of radical proposals dribbling out of the mouths of the Democrats.”
---
|
---
| Ivanka Trump, who has helped the President lead the charge on American jobs, “heralded the new report that unemployment hit a 49-year low by cheering, ‘We’re making it happen,’” Paul Bedard reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=085238bf7a&e=3ee4985eea)_. “In a statement to Washington Secrets, the first daughter said, ‘Our policies continue to deliver more and better jobs for American workers.’ While others mined the report for how groups of Americans are benefiting in the economic surge, Trump has noted that there are now more job openings than those in unemployment lines.”
---
|
---
| “It increasingly looks like FBI chief Jim Comey was in far over his head when it came to counter-espionage. He may have been fooled by Russian disinformation targeting Trump,” the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cc6b55bc79&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “Comey just penned a Times op-ed calling Barr a lying liar who’s been thoroughly corrupted by the president. Sure sounds like he’s worried about what Bill Barr is going to discover in his review of the misadventures of Comey’s FBI.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e0af300ea4&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=50daf03e6e&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=38efd37866)
---

name: Andrew Yang
subject: 3 Rallies in 3 Cities
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=1)[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=2) Hello and thank you for the support! It means a ton to us. This weekend was a lot of fun. We started in Seattle where we were greeted by a crowd of thousands. The rally in Seattle was enormous on a beautiful day. [We made the evening news](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=ONsr6PNq5PuTynREQdFfwk5mfzaaDsTJ7AQYqfBqsoh2PbIZSXbQ44UAebyebOzBd0nKQh1m6haXl8DGQ5ob6MHsjX-uCQIGvnLYeme3Sl-pomfXCTueGUrRAhfAej81NonBjdaQLMAIgO3j_E5mC5tCt9J-z2zxrXrHeoPrfapku7rYfqf-3eRW6_kFKoJH&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=3 "K5 News Story on Andrew Yang's Seattle
Rally"), which is always great. I enjoy the thought of people turning on their evening news and seeing a crowd of thousands of their neighbors rallying for the Freedom Dividend and a more human economy. The YangGang has been strong in Seattle from Day One. It may be because Seattle itself is being transformed by the advent of technology. Housing costs have skyrocketed in certain areas. A friend of mine who works at Amazon said, “It’s no secret that we are trying to make our own jobs obsolete. Management doesn’t pretend otherwise.” A neuroscientist in Seattle said to me, “We need Universal Basic Income in the worst way. The biggest obstacle is the human mind.” He meant that we are wired for resource scarcity. He’s right. Moving people toward abundance is both our greatest challenge and opportunity. Next, it was on to Detroit, which has been on the receiving end of automation. It was the epicenter of hundreds of thousands of manufacturing jobs that have disappeared. Its population has declined from 1.8 million to less than half that. I have spent a lot of time in Detroit—I’ve been there every year since 2011 with Venture for America. The city has made a lot of progress since then. We drew hundreds of people to the Detroit Shipping Company. Among the people I met there were Lacey, a truck driver who sees what is coming down the pike, and Derek, a military vet who has become an activist for mental health issues. I learned a lot from the people of Detroit over the years. They have been doing the hard work building up their neighborhoods and communities. Some people regard Detroit as a symbol of America’s industrial past—it is more accurately an emblem of America’s future as our economy continues to transform. Many towns and communities in the Midwest will increasingly resemble Detroit if we automate truck driving and the trucks don’t need to stop anymore. As Derek said, “It’s incredible what we let happen to Detroit. Half the suburbs in this country are going to look like Detroit if we don’t do something about it.” One issue that is common to Detroit and many other communities—urban blight. It is expensive to tear down buildings properly. If a municipal budget goes down, it becomes almost impossible to remove derelict buildings that then bring down property values and become havens for crime. Detroit alone has about 30,000 derelict homes and buildings, with 30,000 more on the edge of abandonment. One thing that I will do as President is commit Federal funds to a domestic infrastructure force that can help communities clear out or repurpose derelict buildings for redevelopment. We need to become better at helping communities reconfigure themselves in the new economy. It will also create work in many environments. We will be back in Detroit for the DNC debates in July, and I’m excited for it. On Sunday, it was on to Minneapolis. The #YangGang there is strong—1,000 people came out to Boom Island Park. Minnesota has been a blue state for a while, but recently it has become more competitive as many rural voters went to Trump. Minneapolis is something of the progressive haven in the state. I talked about why the folks in other parts of the state seem to be increasingly distressed. They see an economy that doesn’t work for them or their kids as rural areas become more depleted and main street stores close. Minnesota votes early in the primaries on Super Tuesday in March. Also of note—Minneapolis is only 2 hours from the Iowa border. If the #YangGang gets excited enough, we could draw people from Minnesota to campaign in Iowa ahead of the caucuses. We will come back to Minneapolis—St. Paul for sure; I enjoyed the heck out of it. It was invigorating to see so many Americans pumped up about me and the campaign all over the country. We can make the changes that we seek together if we wake up our fellow citizens to the power that we collectively wield. Our campaign is gaining steam all of the time. [We were featured on the Today Show](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=3i22aBYJoJ7A-_x44B3DSwn2NJExmA3c73mp8-PxUBXpRZ9uY8xGQnyXJFpvvWMugAKoSBErWHFn3ZcBou3WXyL9svltAHhKE91J_avCp9megzQ3bFk9KCFj06LQBGeu1h9Pzd26Xo-8mvJIXKg9iw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=4 "Presidential candidate Andrew Yang’s plan for
universal basic income") this Sunday, and I’ll be doing more media this week. I am [heading to New Hampshire later this week](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=5 "Andrew Yang's Humanity
First Rally Schedule"), and then, on May 14th, we will have [our biggest rally yet in Washington Square Park](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghf92CPOI7pOoedoIqdOGAc6xUoSty6OWRxsrXn5Uz0RNzDr1f5Qjsj1hZQfbdRmW3g&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=6 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First
Rally in New York City") in New York City. Thousands of people from around the country will be there. Tell your friends in New York to make the trip—it will be a ton of fun. We are going to bring the media to the rally—let’s build a crowd that will make them stand up and take notice. It’s not Left or Right; it’s Forward. Your forward-looking candidate, \- Andrew
---

[ DONATE $10](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztwOurmyDQdaH3NaKLwEpcO18xGzef5TvO42JaBQed0jXS7z6B0vQBSFYMqiqGORLM&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=7)

[ DONATE $20](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztwOurmyDQdaH3NaKLwEpcO18xGzef5TvO42JaBQed0jdWXtiYd7YoTmFcK-0i3r20&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=8)

[ DONATE $50](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_4EuiRHvEQu_OzjBiZlAGztwOurmyDQdaH3NaKLwEpcO18xGzef5TvO42JaBQed0jaBNWLsD3frblJj2FiCYkZw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=9)

**Can you make a donation today?**

Most of our donations come from people like you chipping in small amounts when they can. Your continued generosity helps us invest in people.

**Join the Yang Gang!**

If you'd like to volunteer to help with grassroots organizing, please fill out an organizer application [here](https://my.yang2020.com/yang_gang_app?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=10) and someone from our team will reach out.

|
---
Thank you for helping to make Universal Basic Income a reality.

[Yang2020.com](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGSZwl_jDGybFpG2O3zBACL4&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=11) | [Twitter](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=YqgukYA3Mrlri-dqG7I8TqE6xdNTSVfW5RhtXxhMjJ0&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=12) | [Facebook](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=27Ax0UmWOX2J8J_QwK9Z6iem_nK8ycftXP-O5PmREpZFFJK2fNzInyXSvMk1KZmg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=13) | [Instagram](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=6hyvAYXBs1PELL28GD73y48vYg4fs9ZaLi2ukqvLwvJQL5wg0UiTTe5otEcf4_Y3&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=05062019_newsletter_2&n=14)

New York, NY

Elizabeth doesn't host any fancy fundraising events where people can only get in if they can write a giant check.

She doesn't take money from federal lobbyists or PACs.

She doesn't take applications from billionaires who could run a Super PAC on her behalf.

**Instead, grassroots contributions from people like you make it possible for Elizabeth to keep fighting this fight.**

We know this means we won't be able to keep up dollar-for-dollar with some of the other campaigns. And that's okay. But it does mean that your contribution is that much more important to our campaign.

**[So if you want to keep Elizabeth and her bold vision front and center in this Democratic primary-- if you want to keep her voice as strong as possible as she fights for working people -- can you chip in to power our grassroots movement?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f8c9/45c30650/804fee13/66962578/2790149651/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsImUzc2dkWEpzSUgxOSI6ImV3LW1heTE5ZnIiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6IjIwMTkwNTA2ZW0xLWZ1bGwifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ew-may19fr?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=20190506em1-full")**

name: John Hickenlooper
subject: [Help Needed]
body:
We need your help to get our campaign's message out there and break through in a historically crowded field. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49138/1630239636/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend, as we enter the lead-up to the first presidential debates, I'm getting specific about my goals as president and my vision of an America that is progressive and welcoming, safe and affordable, and brimming with encouragement and opportunity for our workers, our entrepreneurs, and our middle class.

**I can 't wait to fight for this humane, expansive vision on the debate stage. But that won't happen unless we make our daily progress toward 65,000 Debate Donors. **[**_We need everyone on this team who is able to chip in $1 to do it as soon as possible --can you chip in today?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49139/1630239636/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190506_JH_20190506-fr-JH-econ-ndnr_ndnr_l1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=") **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49136/1630239636/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190506_JH_20190506-fr-JH-econ-ndnr_ndnr_b1&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")** On Friday, I unveiled my detailed plan to create an economy that **rewards workers, reduces income inequality, and promotes opportunity for young people and entrepreneurs. These policies and principles aren 't abstract to me: they flow from my life experiences as a successful small businessman, and I can't wait to put them into action as our next president.**

[**_You can read the comprehensive plan here, but some major priorities include_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49137/1630239636/VEsC/ "https://medium.com/@johnhickenlooper/john-hickenlooper-national-strategy-for-a-working-america-ad9acae25178")** _**:**_**
* Securing a **nationwide minimum wage of $15** that rises with the cost of living
* **Reducing the costs of higher education** (including free community college for all who can't afford it)
* **Infrastructure revival** that prioritizes green jobs and expansion of rural broadband
* **Promoting entrepreneurship** through tax credits and **_protecting_ it** through strong anti-trust enforcement
* Ensuring **affordable, portable health care** options for all
* **Fair, open, principled trade policies** that safeguard labor and environmental standards

Our economy needs to do a far better job of encouraging and rewarding workers and entrepreneurs--and as a former small business owner myself, I'll be ready to put workers and entrepreneurs back at the center of our policies on Day 1 of my presidency.

[**_I can 't wait to share this vision on the debate stage--and a contribution of just $1 will get me closer to the 65,000 donors I need to qualify. Can you step up today?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49134/1630239636/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190506_JH_20190506-fr-JH-econ-ndnr_ndnr_l2&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")

Thank you,

John **[](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a49135/1630239636/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-65kdonors?refcode=em_fr_20190506_JH_20190506-fr-JH-econ-ndnr_ndnr_contribbutton&amount=1&email=&firstname=&lastname=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&occupation=&employer=")**

****__** **__****
---
| | Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
---

PO Box 6377, Denver, CO 80206
Contributions or gifts to Hickenlooper 2020 are not tax deductible.

We love staying connected to you--but if at any point you'd like to opt out, you can do so right [here](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771671/691c9bd/6c6c6d9e/11a4912f/1630239636/VEsHBw/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

subject: NRA Applauds Governor Holcomb for Signing 2nd Amendment Bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Indiana Republican Party
body:
With the NRA's national convention in town, Indiana and Governor Eric Holcomb were once again at center stage. Our state was proud to host the...

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/indiana/nra-applauds-governor-holcomb-for-signing-2nd-amendment-bill?e=1acd9bb3d6)
NRA Applauds Governor Holcomb for Signing 2nd Amendment Bill
Bill Eliminates Fee for Five-Year License to Carry & Guarantees Other Protections

https://www.nraila.org/articles/20190426/nra-applauds-indiana-gov-eric-holcomb-for-signing-comprehensive-gun-rights-bill-into-law?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID]
With the NRA's national convention in town, Indiana and Governor Eric Holcomb were once again at center stage. Our state was proud to host the NRA, and Governor Holcomb made some big news on the group's national stage...literally!

https://www.nraila.org/articles/20190426/nra-applauds-indiana-gov-eric-holcomb-for-signing-comprehensive-gun-rights-bill-into-lawThat's?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID] because when Governor Holcomb took the stage for his speech, he signed a comprehensive new gun rights law (https://www.nraila.org/articles/20190426/nra-applauds-indiana-gov-eric-holcomb-for-signing-comprehensive-gun-rights-bill-into-law?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) that will make it easier for law-abiding Hoosiers to defend themselves!

Standing alongside members of the Indiana General Assembly, Governor Holcomb signed the bill, which will:
* Eliminate fees for the new five-year state License to Carry a Handgun
* Provide greater legal protections for gun owners who exercise their right to self-defense
* Allow gun owners to carry for self-protection in church, if the property owner allows
* Allow gun owners to register to vote when they apply for a License to Carry a Handgun

As Governor Holcomb said on stage, "There’s no place more free or more supportive of the 2nd Amendment than Indiana!"
The NRA is applauding the move, and we do, too! Read what they're saying here! (https://www.nraila.org/articles/20190426/nra-applauds-indiana-gov-eric-holcomb-for-signing-comprehensive-gun-rights-bill-into-law?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

We're so proud that Indiana hosted 80,000 NRA members in our state, attracting a visit from President Donald Trump and Vice President Mike Pence. The convention left an estimated $35 million economic impact on our state.

Thank Governor Holcomb for signing this pro-2nd Amendment legislation by sharing this post on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/587835028017559/posts/1454680691332984/?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) or this post on Twitter (https://twitter.com/HolcombForIN/status/1125432567031828481?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID]) , and let your friends know that our governor stands for the rights of honest, hard-working gun owners across our state!

Read: NRA Applauds Indiana Governor Eric Holcomb (https://www.nraila.org/articles/20190426/nra-applauds-indiana-gov-eric-holcomb-for-signing-comprehensive-gun-rights-bill-into-law?mc_cid=75ae69dc97&mc_eid=[UNIQID])

But Trump 's rapidly working to block Mueller's testimony -- under no circumstances can we let him succeed. ** I need a swift and MASSIVE surge of support before midnight to prove that Americans DEMAND to hear what Mueller has to say. "" Thank you, Ben Ray Lujan | "" "" "" People for Ben PO Box 25371 Albuquerque, NM 87125 "" | | Paid for and authorized by People for Ben.

Going into the 2020 election, it's more important than ever that every eligible American can cast a ballot. Especially in swing states like Michigan -- which Trump narrowly won in 2020 against all odds.
Republicans are vehemently opposed to expanding the right to vote, because they know if they can suppress young people, poor people, and people of color, they increase their chances of winning.
We need to stop them -- and stand up for the right to vote.
We're committed to strengthening the core of our participatory democracy by expanding voting rights and fighting back against Republicans' agenda of suppression.

body:
Thirty years ago, I almost died. | [](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984b15/33d1dea4/3b6f940e/3a3a3f7d/2933978834/VEsH/ "https://romanoff.bsd.net/page/s/volunteer?utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=EM050619&source=EM050619")
---
| | | Thirty years ago, I almost died. My appendix ruptured, and the doctor who removed it said it was the worst he'd ever seen. If I'd gotten to the hospital an hour later, he told my mother, I wouldn't have survived. Many Americans aren't so lucky. Our nation allows thousands of people to die each year because they can't afford health care. **[Join me in the fight for universal coverage.](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984b15/33d1dea4/3b6f940e/3a3a3f7c/2933978834/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020?refcode=EM050619&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=2+-+Join+me+in+the+fight+for+universal+cover&utm_campaign=EM050619&source=EM050619")** As speaker of the Colorado House and as president of Mental Health Colorado, I stood up for Coloradans in need of care. We won those fights even when the insurance industry marshalled an army of lobbyists against us. But much of the progress we've made is now in jeopardy. The Trump Administration and its allies are hellbent on repealing the Affordable Care Act, two years after the House voted to gut it. **[Help me fight back.](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984b15/33d1dea4/3b6f940e/3a3a3f7f/2933978834/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020?refcode=EM050619&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=3+-+Help+me+fight+back&utm_campaign=EM050619&source=EM050619")** I'm running for the U.S. Senate because I don't want anyone to struggle or suffer or die on account of a problem we can fix. We can curb the cost of prescription drugs, protect people with preexisting conditions, and bring affordable, high-quality health care within reach of every American--but we're running out of time. **[Will you stand with me right now?](https://secure.andrewromanoff.com/page/m/7a984b15/33d1dea4/3b6f940e/3a3a3f7e/2933978834/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/romanoff2020?refcode=EM050619&utm_medium=email&utm_source=romanoff&utm_content=4+-+Will+you+stand+with+me+right+now&utm_campaign=EM050619&source=EM050619")** Andrew Romanoff

name: ElizabethWarren.com
subject: Quick survey: What matters most to you in Pennsylvania?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for President
body:
Building a movement doesn't happen by chit-chatting with wealthy donors over champagne and shrimp cocktail. | | | [](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f8c4/45c30650/800c1559/66962dde/3715448525/VEsH/ "https://elizabethwarren.com/")
---
On a typical presidential campaign, the candidate spends a lot of time holding swanky fundraisers in big cities hearing about what issues big donors care about.

But we're doing things differently on this campaign. Because Elizabeth has sworn off fancy fundraisers, her schedule is open so we can invest in the priorities we care about: organizing communities and having person-to-person contact all across the country.

And it also means she gets to go to places our presidential primary process too often leaves behind, including Puerto Rico, Alabama, Mississippi, Tennessee, Utah, and, later this week, West Virginia.

**Elizabeth does this to meet people where they are and talk about the issues that matter to them in their communities and states.**

**[So we want to know: Will you fill out this 2-minute survey and let Elizabeth know what issues matter to you in Pennsylvania?](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f8c4/45c30650/800c1559/66962dc1/3715448525/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwNmVtMi1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-warrenissuessurvey?source=20190506em2-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Elizabeth is running the campaign she wants to run -- because she knows that to make real change, we've got to build a movement.

Building a movement doesn't happen by chit-chatting with wealthy donors over champagne and shrimp cocktail.

And that movement won't happen because of super PACs, or self-funding billionaires, or corporate money. It happens because we'll build it one person, one conversation at a time all across this country.

**That 's why she wants to hear from you and the issues you care about in Pennsylvania.

Fill out our 2-minute survey to tell Elizabeth what issues matter to you in Pennsylvania. Your input is powerfully important:**

**[https://my.elizabethwarren.com/may-issues-survey](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f8c4/45c30650/800c1559/66962dc0/3715448525/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwNmVtMi1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/may-issues-survey?source=20190506em2-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks,

Team Warren
| [**TAKE THE SURVEY**](https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/2852f8c4/45c30650/800c1559/66962dc1/3715448525/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiIyMDE5MDUwNmVtMi1mdWxsLWEifQ==/ "https://my.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-warrenissuessurvey?source=20190506em2-full-a&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

body:
Americans have seen good economies and bad economies over the years, from the stagflation of the 1970s to the Reagan Revival... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2f754e65c4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 6, 2019
---
| | |
# **This time is different**

**Americans have seen** good economies and bad economies over the years, from the stagflation of the 1970s to the Reagan Revival and from the Great Recession to the slow, crawling “recovery” of the Obama Administration.

**The Trump Economy** is roaring, but it’s also different. In previous booms, fast growth often meant soaring stocks and huge returns to those at the very top—the well-connected and well-heeled who got rich off the globalized economy. The stock market continues to break records under President Donald J. Trump—good news for anyone with a 401(k)—but it’s not the upper class who’s seeing the biggest gains in this economy.
**🎬****Watch:** [“We're killing it on the economy!” says Larry Kudlow🔥](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=247101f0ea&e=3ee4985eea)
**“It’s a good time not to be ‘privileged,’** to borrow the political vernacular of our progressive friends,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board wrote on Friday. “Employers added 263,000 jobs in April as the unemployment rate dropped to 3.6%, the lowest in five decades. The best news is that the biggest beneficiaries of this tight labor market are the folks who struggled during the slow-growth Obama years.”

**That means blue-collar** , middle-class, and working-class Americans. Today, the unemployment rate for both recent community college and vocational school graduates is actually _lower_ than it is for grads from four-year colleges. “Less educated Americans are experiencing faster wage and job growth,” the _Journal’s_ editors explain.

**Congressional Democrats** increasingly tell us that a heavy dose of socialism—think massive, [government-run healthcare](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c0be25519&e=3ee4985eea)—is needed to give everybody a chance at the American Dream. That’s an awkward attack on President Trump’s economy when historically marginalized groups are seeing the following results, per Friday’s jobs report:
* The unemployment rate for **adult women** hit 3.1 percent in April, the lowest rate _since 1953_.

* Unemployment for **Hispanics** fell to 4.2 percent, the lowest rate _ever_ since that statistic was first recorded in 1973.

* Unemployment for Americans with only a **high-school degree** fell to 3.5 percent. That matches its lowest rate in nearly two decades.

* Unemployment for those with a **disability** fell to 6.3 percent last month, the lowest rate since that statistic was first recorded in 2008.

**“Believe it or not** —and liberals won’t want to admit it,” _The Wall Street Journal’s_ editors write, “the evidence is that the faster economic growth of the last two years is reducing income inequality.”

[Kudlow: “The strongest jobs growth is from the blue-collar sector.”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2a8349c7ff&e=3ee4985eea)

**Big news:** [The lowest unemployment rate in nearly 50 years!](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4c118deff1&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: The Commander-in-Chief trophy **
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c7ea7ab5f7&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **This afternoon,** President Trump had the honor of presenting the Commander-in-Chief’s Trophy for the second year in a row to the Army Black Knights football team.

**Each year,** the President presents the trophy to the service academy with the best football record against its rival military teams, including the U.S. Naval and Air Force Academies. This marked the first time that West Point has won the honor twice back-to-back.

**“When you play** for Army, you’re taught the courage to take a hit, the strength to sacrifice for your team, and the grit to fight for every single inch,” [President Trump said](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4cc633759e&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
